2605.13652.title: Beyond Perplexity: A Geometric and Spectral Study of Low-Rank Pre-Training
2605.13652.paper_summary: Pre-training large language models is dominated by the memory cost of storing full-rank weights, gradients, and optimizer states. Low-rank pre-training has emerged to address this, and the space of methods has grown rapidly. A central question remains open: do low-rank methods produce models that generalize comparably to full-rank training, or does the rank constraint fundamentally alter the solutions reached? Existing comparisons rely almost entirely on validation perplexity from single-seed runs, often carried forward from prior literature. Yet perplexity is a poor proxy for solution quality; two methods can match on perplexity while converging to different loss landscape regions and internal representations. We close this gap by characterizing the solutions found by five low-rank pre-training methods, GaLore and Fira (memory-efficient optimizers), CoLA and SLTrain (architecture reparameterizations), and ReLoRA (adapter-style updates with periodic resets), against full-rank training at three model scales (60M, 130M, 350M). We evaluate each along 16 metrics across four dimensions: 1-D loss landscape along random/top-K PCA directions, 1-D interpolation between checkpoints, spectral structure of the weights and learned updates, and activation similarity to full-rank training. We show that low-rank methods are not equivalent to full-rank training, nor to one another, even when validation perplexity is close. Full-rank training settles into a sharper basin than low-rank methods along random directions, while the reverse holds for the top-1 PCA direction. Each method converges to a geometrically distinct basin. Low-rank activations diverge from full-rank in later layers as training progresses, with GaLore tracking full-rank most closely. Further, validation perplexity does not translate to downstream performance at every scale. Adding geometric and spectral metrics improves the prediction.
2605.13652.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練大型語言模型主要受到存儲全秩權重、梯度和優化器狀態的記憶體成本的主導。低秩預訓練已經出現以解決這個問題，並且方法的範疇迅速增長。仍然存在一個核心問題：低秩方法是否產生與全秩訓練相當的泛化模型，還是秩的限制根本改變了所達到的解決方案？現有的比較幾乎完全依賴於來自單種子運行的驗證困惑度，這通常是從先前文獻中延續下來的。然而，困惑度對於解決方案質量來說是一個不良的代理；兩種方法可以在困惑度上匹配，同時收斂到不同的損失景觀區域和內部表示。我們通過描述五種低秩預訓練方法（GaLore 和 Fira（記憶體高效的優化器）、CoLA 和 SLTrain（架構重參數化）、以及 ReLoRA（帶有定期重置的適配器風格更新））所找到的解決方案，來填補這一空白，並與三個模型規模（60M、130M、350M）的全秩訓練進行比較。我們在四個維度上沿著16個指標評估每種方法：沿隨機/top-K PCA 方向的1-D損失景觀、檢查點之間的1-D插值、權重和學習更新的光譜結構，以及與全秩訓練的激活相似性。我們顯示低秩方法與全秩訓練並不等同，也彼此之間並不相等，即使驗證困惑度接近。全秩訓練在隨機方向上收斂到一個更尖銳的盆地，而在 top-1 PCA 方向上則相反。每種方法收斂到一個幾何上不同的盆地。隨著訓練的進展，低秩激活在後層與全秩激活發散，GaLore 與全秩的追蹤最為接近。此外，驗證困惑度並不會在每個規模上轉化為下游性能。添加幾何和光譜指標可以改善預測。
2605.13652.author: Namrata Shivagunde et.al.
2605.13652.authors: Namrata Shivagunde, Vijeta Deshpande, Sherin Muckatira, Anna Rumshisky
